title: 3 bedroom houses for rent in Tremont
metaTitle: 3 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Tremont, Cleveland
metaDescription: Searching for 3 bedroom houses for rent in Tremont, Cleveland? See typical rents, historic housing stock, transit, and Section 8-friendly homes here.
primaryKeyword: 3 bedroom houses for rent Tremont cleveland
answerBox: Three-bedroom houses in Tremont, Cleveland's near-west side arts and dining district around Lincoln Park in ZIP 44113, are typically 19th-century frame homes or the larger unit of a converted duplex. Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts median 3-bedroom rent at $1,350; our own recent 3-bedroom Cleveland listings have ranged roughly $1,100–$1,500.

sections.h2: 3-bedroom houses for rent in Tremont, Cleveland
body: Tremont is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44113**, built on a hilltop overlooking the Cuyahoga Valley and centered around Lincoln Park. It's known locally as an arts and dining district, with historic frame houses lining streets that radiate out from the park. For renters who need three bedrooms, Tremont's stock includes single-family homes and two-family duplexes where the larger unit carries the extra bedroom, often on the upper floor.

We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Tremont as part of our broader Cleveland selection. [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability, or [tell our team what you need](/p/book-showing) if a 3-bedroom isn't listed yet.

sections.h2: What does a 3-bedroom home look like in Tremont?
body: Tremont's housing stock dates largely to the late 1800s and early 1900s, built for the industrial workforce of the nearby Cuyahoga Valley steel mills. Homes are typically narrow-lot frame construction, some with hillside or elevated lots that overlook the valley, and many have been subdivided into two-family duplexes over the decades. A 3-bedroom unit here is often either an intact single-family house or the upper unit of a duplex with an extra bedroom carved from a former attic or sunroom.

Because of the neighborhood's age, renters should expect features typical of pre-war Cleveland construction — full basements, gas or hot-water heat, and a detached garage or on-street parking — rather than the central air and attached two-car garages more common in newer suburban rentals. Some homes on Tremont's hillside streets also come with elevated porches or yards that take advantage of the neighborhood's valley-overlook topography.

sections.h2: How much does a 3-bedroom house cost in Tremont?
body: Tremont sits within the City of Cleveland, so citywide figures are the most reliable published benchmark for this ZIP. Zumper's report dated July 4, 2026 lists the City of Cleveland's median 3-bedroom asking rent at $1,350 a month. RentCafe's report dated July 2, 2026, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment communities, lists an average 3-bedroom rent of $2,716 for roughly 1,483 square feet — higher than Zumper's figure because it reflects newer buildings rather than Tremont's older housing stock. Our own recently available 3-bedroom homes across Cleveland have generally rented from about $1,100 to $1,500 a month; check current availability for Tremont specifically, since it changes.

rows: Citywide asking-rent median, all listing types

body: Tremont sits between the Cuyahoga Valley (the Flats) and I-490/I-71, with the Abbey Avenue and Jennings Freeway routes providing direct access toward Downtown Cleveland and the West 25th–Ohio City Red Line station a short distance north. Several GCRTA bus routes connect the neighborhood to Downtown and to Ohio City. Lincoln Park, at the neighborhood's core, is the primary green space, with the Professor Avenue and W. 14th Street corridors carrying most of the local restaurant and retail activity.

For a household renting a 3-bedroom home, Tremont's mix of highway access (I-490/I-71) and nearby rail (the Ohio City Red Line station) can support commutes to Downtown, the West Side, or destinations further out via I-71 without requiring a home directly on a bus line.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for 3-bedroom homes in Tremont
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including our 3-bedroom listings. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and the City of Cleveland has not enacted a local source-of-income protection, so not every landlord in Tremont will accept a voucher — ours do. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how CMHA vouchers and payment standards work, or compare with [houses for rent in Ohio City](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/), Tremont's neighboring near-west side district.

faq.q: Are there 3-bedroom houses for rent in Tremont, Cleveland?
faq.a: Yes. Tremont's housing stock includes single-family homes and duplexes with 3-bedroom configurations, often the larger unit of a converted two-family building. Availability changes regularly, so book a showing or tell our team what you need and we'll follow up with current options.

faq.a: Zumper's July 2026 report puts the City of Cleveland's median 3-bedroom rent at $1,350/month. Our own recently available 3-bedroom homes across Cleveland have typically ranged from about $1,100 to $1,500, depending on condition and location within the neighborhood.
